title: Can I give an AI app $100 in seed money and have them day-trade for me 100% of the time and pay me 50% of their net profits every day that the stock markets are open?
author: DunDonese
selftext:
Can I give an AI app $100 in seed money and have them day-trade for me 100% of the time and pay me 50% of their net profits every day that the stock markets are open?

What apps auto-invest and auto-daytrade for me with a small startup capital such as only $100? What are the pros & cons of those apps? Is there anything else I need to do besides sign up and transfer to said AI automated investment app $100 to get them started on day trading?

comments.author: jabberw0ckee
comments.body:
You will be able to do this at StockKit.ai once our Alpaca integration is done in a few weeks.

https://www.reddit.com/r/ai\_trading/s/ER5OXlwfyf

Elite Tier enables auto trading

comments.author: kwame1776
comments.body: I recommend you review and understand how the app is using AI to trade. Don’t simply sign for any black boxes that promise to use “advanced AI” - you’ll be throwing money away. Find out what exactly the AI is doing and how it comes up with trade ideas. If it makes sense to you, then go ahead.

comments.author: xXDADDYTHRASHERXx
comments.body: I have been building a system like this for the past year and it only survives in 2 types of regimes.  This is not impossible idea. But its not just tell it what to do and walk away. it requires constant work.  it takes patience, persistence, discipline and understanding of the market.  Do it!  just understand it wont print money overnight.

comments.author: Objective-Part-2346
comments.body: Challenging - I don't think such App exists. Why would they sell it? Better to keep it for yourself and move on.... How do you trust the App? There is big question on trust and compliance. What would you say if they get back to you with 80% loss in one day? Do you believe them? Do you believe that they didn't really invest and kept the money for them? Very difficult I think.

comments.author: No-Consequence-1779
comments.body:
Do you see that much movement in your normal trading that would even reach that?  Probably not. 

So yes. Ai can do anything. Just AI it!

comments.author: Ok_Freedom3290
comments.body:
**Great question — and the short answer is: sort of, but not quite the way you're imagining it.**

Let me break this down properly because there's a lot of nuance here that most "just sign up!" articles won't tell you.

**The $100 Reality Check**

Most regulated brokers have pattern day trader (PDT) rules that require a $25,000 minimum to execute more than 3 day trades per week in US equities. So with $100, you're largely confined to crypto markets — which are actually where most AI trading bots operate anyway since they're 24/7 and unregulated in that respect.

**What actually exists:**

* **Copy trading platforms** (eToro, Bybit) — you follow other traders, not AI
* **Crypto bots** (3Commas, Pionex) — rule-based automation, not true AI. DCA, grid, and RSI-trigger strategies. They don't "pay you profits" — they just trade your capital
* **Robo-advisors** (Betterment, Wealthfront) — long-only, passive rebalancing. Not day trading at all

**The "50% of profits" model doesn't exist at $100.** That's a hedge fund structure (2/20). No app is going to split gains with you — they charge subscription fees or take a % of your AUM.

**The honest cons nobody mentions:**

* AI bots can lose money just as fast as they make it, especially in choppy sideways markets
* Fees eat small accounts alive
* Slippage on $100 trades can wipe your edge entirely
* Most "AI trading" is just backtested strategies that break in live conditions

**What actually works at small scale:**

If you want genuine signal intelligence — understanding *why* a trade is being flagged, what the funding rates look like, where institutional money is flowing — that's where purpose-built terminals like [AlphaSignal](https://alphasignal.digital/) come in. It's not a bot that blindly fires trades; it surfaces the institutional-grade signals (RSI exhaustion zones, Z-score anomalies, whale activity) and you decide. At $100, staying in control of your execution is actually your biggest edge over a blind bot.

**TL;DR:** Automate the *analysis*, keep control of the *execution* until you have enough capital to absorb bot errors. At $100, a bot mistake is your whole account.
